Mohammed

entry.title: Public cloud transport from on premise 1909
entry.content:
Hi,

Please advise 

Our company creates and transfers a development transport (CR) containing ABAP code,  which is compatible to S4/HANA 1909 on premise

technically will we be able to transfer this same transport if we try to do so to  SAP Public Cloud 2402 system ?  means does public cloud support importing transports from S/4 HANA on premise ?

 

Best Regards,

Gal Kogan

entry.title: Re: How to include a URL to access the SAP WF approval sc...
entry.content: Thank you very much for your response. I understand that URL configuration is required in advance. I understand that the following steps will achieve this: The URL is generated according to the URL configuration content. The generated URL is stored in I_WorkflowTaskURL (key item: Workflow ID). The URL retrieved from I_WorkflowTaskURL is included in the approval request email body. Regarding step 3, is my understanding correct that the key item, Workflow ID, can be obtained from the following in the workflow container?

entry.title: ABAP RAP(RESTful Application Programming) Model OData V2 API Deep Entity
entry.content:
In this blog, We will learn how to create a simple RAP OData V2 API and use it to achieve a Deep entity set creation operation which is requested in lot of projects and Custom services especially in complex integration scenarios without need to Create normal CDS entity with selection from DB Tables .

In this example,  CDS Custom entity views for Header and Items , Unmanaged Behavior definition, Service definition, Service Binding . 

Step #1 - > Create Custom Entity - Root Header

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Custom Entity - Root Header'
define root custom entity ZCE_R_Header
{
   key hdr_Key   : land1;
       hdr_field1: landx;
       hdr_field2: natio;
       
    _item : composition [1..*] of ZCE_I_Items ;    
}

 

Step #2 - > Create Custom Entity - for Items 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Custom Entity - Items'
define custom entity ZCE_I_Items
{
  key hdr_Key      : land1;
  key itm_key      : bukrs;
      Company_name : butxt;
  
   _header : association to parent ZCE_R_Header on $projection.hdr_Key = _header.hdr_Key;
}

 

Step #3 - > Right Click on Custom Entity ZCE_R_Header and Create Behavior Definition 

Step #4 -> Unmanaged Behaviour creation as below with Unmanaged Class implementation 

 

unmanaged implementation in class zCL_bp_ce_r_header unique;
//strict ( 2 ); //Uncomment this line in order to enable strict mode 2. The strict mode has two variants (strict(1), strict(2)) and is prerequisite to be future proof regarding syntax and to be able to release your BO.

define behavior for ZCE_R_Header //alias <alias_name>
//late numbering
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly : update ) hdr_Key;
  association _item { create; }
}

define behavior for ZCE_I_Items //alias <alias_name>
//late numbering
lock dependent by _header
authorization dependent by _header
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) hdr_Key,itm_key;
  association _header;
}

 

Step #5 -> Create Behaviour Implementation Class Save it and Activate .

Step #6 -> Implement Method cba_itm

Step #7 ->  Create Service Definition 'ZCE_R_Service_Defination'

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Service Defination'
define service ZCE_R_Service_Defination {
  expose ZCE_R_Header as header;
  expose ZCE_I_Items  as items;
}

 

Step #8 -> Create Service Binding

Step #9 -> Publishing Service 

Step #10 -> Register and Activate SICF Node 

Step #11 -> Use below Json Payload - with Post Operation

{
  "hdr_Key"     : "T" ,
  "hdr_field1"  : "hdr_field1" ,
  "hdr_field2"  : "hdr_field2",
  "to_item" :
    [
      {
        "hdr_Key" : "01" ,
        "itm_key" : "01" ,
        "Company_name" : "Company_name1"
      },
      {
         "hdr_Key" : "02" ,
         "itm_key" : "02" ,
         "Company_name" : "Company_name2"
      }
    ]
}

Conclusion :  

we Will Find Our Deep structure data inside ENTITIES_CBA

entry.title: SAP Yard Logistics - Chapter 1
entry.content:
This blog post will discuss how to implement SAP Yard Logistics in your company. Since you already have SAP YL, which is an add-on that you can purchase separately from SAP, please remember that SAP YL is always an add-on for your Embedded S4 HANA system. It is important to note that SAP Yard Logistics is designed to optimize yard operations, such as managing trailers and containers, improving efficiency, and reducing costs. By integrating SAP YL with your existing SAP system, you can streamline processes and enhance visibility across your supply chain.  

Many features set SAP YL apart from SAP EWM Yard Management. With SAP EWM Yard Management, you can only manage your warehouse at a very basic level. In contrast, with SAP YL, you can plan your schedules, loading and unloading procedures, and yard operations with feature-rich features like real-time process optimization and monitoring (e.g Cleaning process, TU Quality check, Movements, loading/unloading and so on).

The process will become fantastic for your business when your YL meets with DAS, EWM, and TM.

I wanted to specifically inform you all that your YL can be linked to EWM TM and PM.

Remember that your SAP YL warehouse will utilize the foundation of EWM and operate on top of its core functionality when you begin implementing SAP YL. This will help you avoid confusion with EWM warehouse.

Since you are likely implementing SAP YL in your system for the first time, please confirm that you have completed the setup listed below in order for SAP YL to function.

Let's find out the secret. I will discuss SAP YL configuration and process chapter by chapter in my next blogs.

Configuration

 

Maintain Text Schema

One of the manuals SAP YL provides for preserving and sharing texts from older documents is text schemas.

 

Maintain Attachment Type Schema

SAP YL We deal with attachments related to business documents on a daily basis. You have the ability to store, view, download, and print attachment files.

Define Warehouse Numbers

The warehouse for SAP YL is different from the SAP EWM warehouse; if you are integrating SAP YL with SAP EWM, you should use a different name in the SAP YL warehouse; otherwise, you will not be able to connect your EWM warehouse and YL warehouse. As I previously stated, don't get confused with warehouse creation if you are from EWM.

Define Warehouse Number Control

These are the essential details that you must provide in order for the warehouse to function (weights, measurements, time, volume, etc).

 

 

 

Create Supply chain unit-  /N/SCMB/SCUMAIN

Assign Warehouse Numbers

Define Yard Numbers - SAP YL WH

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request, Yard Order, Yard Task

Maintain Below-

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request

Define Number Ranges for Yard Order

Define Number Ranges for Yard Task

Define Checkpoints

Define Transportation Unit Types

Define Number Range Interval for Transportation Units

Define Number Ranges for S&R Activities

Maintain Means of Transport

Define Control Parameters for Forming Vehicles/Transportation Units

Define Order Types

Assign Order Type and Threshold for Arrival

Define Date and Time Record Types

 

 

 

Define Warehouse Process Type

Define Activity Types

Define Default Activity Types

 

 

 

 

Define Storage Type

Define Storage Section

Define Storage Bin Types

Load Storage Bin

Goto NEBC à Master Data

Assign Checkpoint to Yard Bin and SCU (/SCWM/YM_CHKPT_BIN)

NWBC – Master Data

Link - Packaging Mat. (TU) and MTr (/SAPYL/PM_MTR)

NWBC – Master Data

Define Default Location for Yard Order/Yard Request (/SAPYL/DEF_LOCAT)

Define Number Ranges for Yard Request, Yard Order, Yard Task

Basic Test –

 

 

 

 

Save It, System will create Yard Request with Status as – Created

You need to Release the Yard Request to Create Yard Order Automatically.

 

System has created Yard Order Automatically and All the Task which was assigned with Yard Order in Configuration are available in the Yard Order.

Thanks For Reading my blogs.

Thanks & Regards,

Muhammad Ashraf

entry.title: Putaway Strategies in Extended Warehouse management part 1
entry.content:
Putaway:

It refers to the process of moving goods from a receiving area to their designated storage locations within the warehouse. It involves the following steps:

  1. Identification: The system identifies where the goods should be stored based on various factors such as item type, size, storage conditions, and available space.
  2. Task Creation: SAP EWM creates a putaway task, which includes instructions on where the goods should be moved. This task is typically generated automatically based on warehouse rules and strategies.
  3. Execution: Warehouse personnel or automated systems execute the putaway task. This involves physically transporting the goods from the receiving area to the assigned storage location.
  4. Confirmation: Once the goods are in the designated storage location, the task is confirmed in SAP EWM, updating inventory records and ensuring accurate stock levels.
  5. Integration: The putaway process integrates with other warehouse processes, such as inventory management and order fulfillment, to maintain an accurate and efficient warehouse operation.

Putaway in SAP EWM aims to optimize storage space, improve inventory accuracy, and enhance overall warehouse efficiency.

Putaway Strategies:

Putaway strategies in SAP EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) are predefined rules and methods used to determine the optimal storage location for goods within a warehouse. These strategies are crucial for optimizing space utilization, ensuring efficient inventory management, and improving warehouse operations.

Effective warehouse management is critical to supply chain efficiency. SAP EWM offers various putaway strategies to streamline operations and enhance inventory management. This article explores nine key putaway strategies: Manual Entry, Fixed Storage Bin, General Storage, Addition to Existing Stock, Empty Storage Bin, Bulk Storage, Pallet Storage, Near Fixed Picking Bin, and Flexible Storage.

Manual Entry:

The manual entry putaway strategy has been examined in several studies, highlighting its flexibility and usefulness in specific situations. Manual entry is advantageous in cases where automated systems may not be ideal, such as with delicate or high-value items. However, this approach can be time-consuming and susceptible to human error, making thorough training and careful oversight essential to maintain accuracy.

Fixed Storage Bin:

Fixed storage bin strategies are well-regarded for their efficiency in optimizing both picking and putaway processes. Assigning dedicated bins to specific items notably reduces search times and improves order accuracy. This approach proves especially effective in settings with consistent inventory levels and predictable demand.

General Storage:

General storage strategies focus on maximizing space utilization by permitting items to be placed in any available location. This flexible approach is particularly advantageous in warehouses with fluctuating inventory levels. General storage can adjust to stock variations, making it ideal for dynamic warehouse settings. However, maintaining accurate inventory and ensuring efficient retrieval can be challenging, necessitating sophisticated warehouse management systems and effective inventory tracking methods.

Adding to existing stock:

Adding to existing stock focuses on consolidating similar items within the same bin to optimize space and streamline inventory management. This approach reduces the need for extra storage space and decreases the time spent locating items. Nonetheless, to avoid overfilling and ensure effective use of storage space, accurate inventory tracking and regular bin audits are essential.

Process Flow:

1) Manual Entry:

The system cannot use manual entry to identify a storage section or bin. You manually enter the destination storage bin once the warehouse task is created or validated. This process is typically used when a warehouse employee searches for an appropriate storage container on-site.

Maintain the following storage type configuration settings to implement the manual entry putaway strategy:

  1. Storage Behavior: standard warehouse
  2. Putaway Rules: next empty bin or additions to existing stock/empty bin
  3. WT Generic: only storage type

Benefits: Flexibility, suitability for special items, and improved handling accuracy.


2) Fixed Storage Bin: 

Using this method, you store a product in bins that are specifically designated for it. This tactic is mostly applied to storage, where picking is done by hand. The system can identify an appropriate storage bin and assign it to the product as a fixed storage bin if you wish to store a product without a fixed bin assignment in a storage type for which you have set a fixed storage bin strategy. A warehouse product in SAP EWM may have one or more permanent bins assigned to it. The system may automatically delete the assignment. A setting in the warehouse product master and storage type may restrict the number of bins allotted to a warehouse product.

Make the following storage type configuration settings to implement the fixed bin strategy:

  1. Storage Behavior: standard warehouse.
  2. Putaway Rules: addition to existing stock/empty bin.
  3. Addition To Stock Forbidden: addition to existing stock permitted.
  4. Set the Use Fixed Storage Bins indicator.
  5. Set the Max. Fixed Bins field if this control is required.
  6. Activate a form of capacity-checking.

Benefits: Enhanced picking efficiency, reduced search times, and improved inventory accuracy.


3) General Storage: 

The system employs the general storage technique to locate a storage bin in a general storage area. You designate one storage bin per section using the general storage putaway approach. Mixed storage is another option for the quants in the storage bin. Maintain the following storage type configuration settings to implement the general storage putaway strategy:

  1. Storage Behavior: standard warehouse
  2. Putaway Rules: general storage area
  3. Mixed Storage: mixed storage without limitations

Benefits: Improved space utilization and flexibility in handling varying inventory levels.


4) Addition to Existing Stock: 

The system attempts to store inventory using this method in storage bins already filled with the same product. Before adding to the current stock, the storage bin must have enough free capacity. If it cannot locate one that contains the same product or does not have enough free capacity, the system looks for the next empty storage bin. This tactic goes against the FIFO concept.

Maintain the following storage type configuration settings to implement the addition to the existing storage strategy:

  1. Storage Behavior: standard warehouse
  2. Putaway Rules: addition to existing stock/empty bin
  3. Addn.Stock Forbidden: To allow an addition to existing stock, you can choose “ ” (Addition to existing stock permitted) or “M” (Product Putaway Profile Decides). If you choose M, you must define profiles according to your requirements and assign them to the warehouse products.
  4. Mixed Storage: only one HU is allowed per bin.
  5. Configure a method of capacity checking.

Benefits: Streamlined inventory management and reduced search times.


Kindly refer Putaway Strategies in Extended Warehouse management part 2 article for the remaining putaway strategy

entry.title: Re: Do Virtual Tables support ST_POINT or ST_GEOMETRY
entry.content:
There are a number of functions which might be of use.

select ll.ST_AsText() as back2text , ll, toText from (
select ST_GeomFromText( totext ) as ll , toText from (
SELECT NEW ST_Point( 1.0, 2.0 ).ST_AsText() as toText FROM dummy));

Not sure of whether accuracy is maintained during the conversion.

So in the source system maybe set up a view (or table ) of the transformed data to text and then connect a VT to this view and then convert back to GEO.

I'll give it a go - answering my own question yet again.

entry.title: Re: Realtime monitoring in warehouse on distant planet
entry.content:
Great project and blog @elnovi 👏

I definitely need to explore ABAP Push Channels + Messaging Channels.

entry.title: Re: Consuming SAP Datasphere business content in differen...
entry.content:
1. Export the required analytical models, along with all dependent objects, as a JSON file from the DEV space.
Ensure all connections, tables, views, and other dependent artifacts are included in the export.
2. Import the JSON file into the PROD space.
After import, you might need to adjust connections or configurations specific to the PROD environment, such as updating the connection to point to the PROD S/4HANA system.

If you are deploying the same business content across multiple spaces (e.g., DEV and PROD), consider setting up a version control strategy. This ensures that any changes made in DEV are tracked and consistently applied to PROD, reducing the risk of inconsistencies.

entry.title: Re: SAP BW on HANA data tiering
entry.content:
create a open ods view / virtual provider that connect to the third party tool. Thai will be cold data.
Create a composite provider that combines the cold data from the ods view and the hot data from current dso.

the Bw quey should automatically control the execution plan and fetch the required data from hot/cold datasets depending on what you are trying to fetch.

entry.title: Re: Post Asset Expense

You may refer to the tile 'Asset Transactions' or you may also explore the tile 'Asset Accounting Overview' which has a bunch of cards including 'Asset Transactions'.

entry.title: Re: Outlook interview scheduling - M365 Mail.ReadWrite &...
entry.content:
HI @delacourt_l 

I'm not a Recriuting Expert but we have an integration between SF LMS and MS Teams.

Find more information here:  Microsoft Graph permissions reference - Microsoft Graph | Microsoft Learn

You'll need to involve the responsible Microsoft Administrators of your organization.

In my understanding only the Recruiting administrators, who schedule candidate interviews, will trigger the two mentioned API to send out the e-mails and interview meeting requests to the candidates. No user without permissions for Recruiting will have the possibility to trigger these API (because the API integration is "protected" by the Client Secret).

I hope this helps.

Good Luck and KR Jonas

entry.title: Re: Stock on Hand using 2LIS_03_BF
entry.content:
To calculate stock on hand for a specific date, you need to aggregate the relevant quantities and values up to that date.

sample sql below which could have some syntax error. Pls Use it as pseudo logic .

For Inventory Quantity:

• Sum the quantities from 2LIS_03_BF (MENGE) for relevant movement types (like 101 for receipts, 201 for issues).
• Ensure you consider the sign (positive for receipts, negative for issues).
• Group by Material Number (MATNR), Plant (WERKS), and Storage Location (LGORT).

SELECT MATNR, WERKS, LGORT, SUM(CASE WHEN BWART IN ('101', '102', '561') THEN MENGE
WHEN BWART IN ('201', '202', '261') THEN -MENGE
ELSE 0 END) AS INVENTORY_QTY
FROM 2LIS_03_BF
WHERE BUDAT <= @input_date
GROUP BY MATNR, WERKS, LGORT;

For Inventory Unit Cost and Inventory Value:

• The 2LIS_03_UM extractor will provide revaluation data, which can be used to calculate the inventory value and cost.
• Combine this data with inventory quantities from 2LIS_03_BF.

SELECT A.MATNR, A.WERKS, A.LGORT,
SUM(A.INVENTORY_QTY * B.DMBTR / B.MENGE) AS INVENTORY_VALUE,
SUM(B.DMBTR / B.MENGE) AS INVENTORY_UNIT_COST
FROM
(
SELECT MATNR, WERKS, LGORT,
SUM(CASE WHEN BWART IN ('101', '102', '561') THEN MENGE
WHEN BWART IN ('201', '202', '261') THEN -MENGE
ELSE 0 END) AS INVENTORY_QTY
FROM 2LIS_03_BF
WHERE BUDAT <= @input_date
GROUP BY MATNR, WERKS, LGORT
) A
JOIN
(
SELECT MATNR, BWKEY, BWTAR, DMBTR, MENGE
FROM 2LIS_03_UM
WHERE BUDAT <= @input_date
) B
ON A.MATNR = B.MATNR;

For In-Network In-Transit Qty and Value:

• To calculate in-transit quantities and values, filter on the movement types that represent in-transit movements.
• Sum the relevant quantities and values from 2LIS_03_BF.

SELECT MATNR, WERKS, LGORT,
SUM(CASE WHEN BWART = '641' THEN MENGE ELSE 0 END) AS IN_TRANSIT_QTY,
SUM(CASE WHEN BWART = '641' THEN MENGE * UNIT_COST ELSE 0 END) AS IN_TRANSIT_VALUE
FROM
(
SELECT MATNR, WERKS, LGORT,
BWART, MENGE,
(SELECT SUM(DMBTR) FROM 2LIS_03_UM WHERE MATNR = M.MATNR AND BUDAT <= @input_date) /
(SELECT SUM(MENGE) FROM 2LIS_03_BF WHERE MATNR = M.MATNR AND BUDAT <= @input_date) AS UNIT_COST
FROM 2LIS_03_BF M
WHERE BUDAT <= @input_date
) X
GROUP BY MATNR, WERKS, LGORT;

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud Output Management – Customize Master Form for Logo and Texts (Footer Blocks)
entry.content:

As per my requirement, I have modified the content Form and used a custom service by redefine the standard ODATA service. and in Configuration mentioned the custom form template name and master form template in determination rule. But still not able to get any logo in my form only printing the content form data and the lines which is available in master form 
Not able to get any logo and texts. Please suggest or let me know any further information need to answer my doubt.

Regards,

Rajeshwari

entry.title: Re: Upgrading to Latest Goal Management – Cycle Planning & Preparedness – Best Practice Approach
entry.content:
Hello,

In our performance process, the Get Feedback functionality is used to give the Get Feedback provider the same editing options in the form as the manager.

We have switched to Latest Goal management in production. The Get Feedback giver is authorized to the Target and the Development template per RBP. The RBP have not been changed.

In Legacy Goal Management, both performance and development goals could be entered via Get Feedback using the form.
This is no longer possible after the changeover to Latest Goal Management.

As the development plan is visible in our company, it is not possible to authorize all employees to all goal/development plans.

This means that the Get Feedback function for creating and editing goals via the form is no longer available.

We urgently need to restore this functionality.

Thank you for your feedback.

Thanks

Conny

entry.title: Re: What is SAP Approuter and its importance ?
entry.content: The standalone approuter can also be deployed on Kyma.

entry.title: Re: Simplest method to create a customizing table with radio buttons/checkboxes
entry.content:
That is a very nice guide to custom config tables. A couple of comments from my side:

Enhancement category

You have suggested to use the fist one "Can Be Enhanced (Deep)"

In my opinion "Cannot Be Enhanced" should be used for custom config tables. The reason is that if you need to add more fields to a table, you will just add them directly to the table definition and won't create an enhancement structure. There is really no point in using enhancement structure for custom tables.

Buffering

You have missed the crucial setting of Table Buffering of Database Tables, e.g. of T001W buffering settings:

The rule of thumb is that buffering should be activated for config tables, either generic area of fully buffered.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: you think like me

entry.title: Issues with Master Data Transfer from ERP HANA Cloud to DM
entry.content:
Dear Members,

 

I hope you are doing well. I am writing to seek your assistance regarding some issues I've encountered while transferring master data from ERP HANA Cloud to Digital Manufacturing (DM).

FYI, I am using DM TDD, HANA Cloud Demo.

 

Issue 1: Missing BOM Information in Routing

According to the documentation, transferring the master routing should update the Bill of Materials (BOM) in DM as well. However, in DM, the BOM information is missing from the routing. I used the Replicate by Object Selection app in ERP HANA Cloud, selecting "Production Bill of Operations" as 'Business Object Type' to transfer the routing to DM. Despite BOM and Production Version being already created in HANA Cloud, the BOM information is not reflected in the routing within DM.

 

 

Issue 2: Excessive Creation of BOM and Routing

Another issue has surfaced concerning the creation of Production Orders. After generating and releasing a Production Order, it correctly reaches DM. Although the BOM does not download to DM, the routing does. However, for each order sent to DM, BOM and routing seem to be created again by accumulation within DM, leading to an excessive number of BOMs and routings.

 

Could you please provide guidance or suggest any solutions to address these issues?

Thank you in advance for your support.

 

Best regards,

Wonpyo

 

SAP HANA Cloud SAP Digital Manufacturing

entry.title: Re: RPA SMTP Mail Destination Configuration with OAuth2Password for Microsoft 365?
entry.content: If the idea is helpful, please develop the project.

entry.title: Re: Teams VLS for users without company email
entry.content:
Hi @EricAbrams 

To be honest, the solution you are looking for ist not "best practice". 😉 

The most important point for a successfull VLS integration with MS Teams is, that the user records of the instructors really exist in Microsoft Azure. If there's no active MS Azure User of the instructor, there will be a validation error if you try to schedule a VLS time slot for a class. If you have external instructors they also need a Microsoft licence AND a MS Azure User of your organization - otherwise you will also get a validation error.

Once you managed to send out a VLS meeting request to at least one user, you can copy the Teams session link from the instructor's or student's Teams calendar and provide it (offline) to other persons. Everyone with the link will be able to join the VLS session, but as I mentioned above, it's not "best practice". 😉

Good luck! KR Jonas

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content: Thanks for your contribution, thoughtful article

entry.title: Re: Hello

entry.title: Re: Aggregation of rate elements with rate subelements Su...
entry.content:
Hi Carsen,

thank you very much again for your explanation, I'm glad that at least I was going in the correct direction in terms of configuration.
Concerning the aggregation, I guess I was confused by the explanatory example proposed in the SAP Help (https://help.sap.com/docs/PRICE_CALCULATION/53987d38467243e28cbeb8514d015666/8dace9fbf88541549e82e2484fa7bd8c.html) where the aggregation is applied to the scenario "You want to price separately different sorts of apples, but the price per kilogram depends on the total quantity of apples in the priceable document."
Thanks again and have a nice day!

Kr,
Elena

entry.title: Re: Custom report in Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi @AngelaDai 

Thanks for your answer.

The blog you mentioned has not specific steps to create custom report. 

Is there any other blogs which contain specific steps?

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards,

KhaingZin

entry.title: S4/Hana Group Reporting: Correct data on Soctypes for Reclassifications
entry.content:
Hi All. Within Group Reporting we have changed some reclassification rules during the year (e.g. changed Plug Accounts from long term to short term elimination). 

Problem is that in the periods that are already closed, the eliminations have obviously run against the old rules. We don't want to reopen those periods so we want to book a correction later in the year. What is the best practice to do so, ideally without posting the correction on a different document type? Should we temporaraly make the document type a manual correction document type to book the manual correction?

 

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Hello
entry.content: Can you help me?

entry.title: Re: SlipStream Issue with QM02

Cheers

James

entry.title: setFilename dynamically in TPM for AS2 configuration
entry.content:
Hi All, 

The scenario is IDOC to AS2 using TPM, and I have the requirement to add filename dynamically in the receiver AS2 channel. For example, the format for adding the filename is message.messageId

Is there any way to add AS2 filename dynamically in TPM configuration? 

Thanks 

Radhika

entry.title: Re: MDG DQM:Employing rules in central governance & mass processing in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi @tachibanay ,

The additional context parameters come as part of SAP note 3344850 - MDQ Performance: Enhancement of BRFplus Context for Validation Rules.

The note is downported till S/4 HANA 2021.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere News in July

entry.title: Re: Extract peer SSL certificate automatically to resolve SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED
entry.content:
ICM level 3 trace logs the server SSL certificate. 
Openssl can also extract SSL certificate from the target with the -showcerts option.

I am a Basis admin . I find having the root certificate in the ABAP trust store more manageable than having the certificate chain if everyone agrees to it. It lasts longer as well in most cases well over 10 years
Specially with the BTP end points, SAP keeps changing the certificates and its recommended to have the root certificate in trust store

entry.title: Purchase requisation firmed indicator check mark selected in MD04 in SAP ECC . it shows in IBP fir
entry.content:
Purchase requisition firmed indicator check mark selected in MD04 in SAP ECC. it shows in IBP firmed and it is converted into conformed transport receipts. even though no one changed in the item change for the product moreover creator indicator is SAP APO.

can you please tell me where can i check who changed it.

kind regards,

Vijender

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )

Cheers,

Dean

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP
entry.content: I have admired rap music for a long time.

entry.title: Re: MRS, Work Manager & Asset Manager
entry.content:
Hi Mohammed, hope you are not confusing the solutions with each other too much, because:

  • MRS (Multiresource Scheduling) is a planning & scheduling solution. It requires installation as an ABAP addon on ERP (both ECC and S4 versions are available, and basically work the same, with just one minor difference regarding the material master, documented here: https://me.sap.com/notes/0002493452 ). BUT: the solution is in maintenance mode and no longer a strategic solution. The current mainstream maintenance end is planned for 31.12.2027 with an option for extended maintenance to 31.12.2030 on ECC.
    Successor solutions of MRS are dependent on the scenario you are trying to address:
    • for (inhouse) asset-centric scenarios, you might want to check SAP S/4HANA Asset Management for resource scheduling (RSH): https://www.sap.com/rsh
    • for service-centric or field-related scenarios or dispatching on technician level, you might want to check SAP Field Service Management (FSM): https://www.sap.com/fsm 
    • for project-staffing scenarios, you might want to check SAP Cloud for projects resource management: https://www.sap.com/products/scm/project-resource-management.html 
  • Work Manager is the predecessor of Asset Manager or Service and Asset Manager respectively, which is a solution for mobile maintenance execution. See the product overview page here: https://www.sap.com/products/scm/asset-manager.html

Hope this helps!
Best regards,

Markus

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: nice bro antifan

entry.title: Can customers expand default memory size?
entry.content:
Hello ILM experts,

As described in the help portal page for ILM Advisor app, the default licensed memory size is 100 GB.
Is this applicable for any customers? If so, can a customer request to expand the memory size more than 100 GB? I guess the customer needs to create a case from SAP for me. What is the component for the case?

Additionally, what is the maximum size for the licensed memory? I found a description regarding the maximum memory size chart by FUEs in the "GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Use Descriptions".

Is this also applicable for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

entry.title: Re: sap S/4HANA SERVICE - REQUEST MANAGEMENT
entry.content:
Hi Dean,

 

Thank you for your response.. 

In my project I am trying to see if I can achieve the below flow-

Now that CS will be a sunset module and S/4Hana service is the future.. wanted to understand if in plant maintenance it can be integrated and could leverage the new functionalities of S/4Hana service. Like would I be able to use the service requests in revisions then create build orders, PM confirmation etc.

entry.title: Re: Cancel Goods Receipt PO query report into SAP Busines...
entry.content:
This query shows goods receipts that were cancelled in the selected period:

SELECT DISTINCT T2.DocEntry,  T2.[DocDate], T2.CardCode, T2.CardName, T0.DocDate as "Cancelled on" 
FROM OPDN T0 
INNER JOIN PDN1 t1 ON t0.Docentry = T1.DocEntry 
INNER JOIN OPDN T2 ON T1.BaseEntry = T2.DocEntry AND T1.BaseType = 20
WHERE T0.[CANCELED] ='C'
AND T0.DocDate > = '[%0]' AND T0.DocDate <= '[%1]'

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)的架构理解
entry.content: 感谢Alex的精彩分享。

entry.title: Re: SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit a new sales transaction validation industry cloud solut
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit (OSTA) can only be used with the Retail Datamodel in the S/4 system. SAP Landscapes without the industry solution IS for Retail will not be able to connect to OSTA for the data transfer (material and billing documents) and also not for the master data synchronisation.

entry.title: Re: Entering SAP Signavio certification
entry.content:
Dear Hana,

I am currently preparing for the SAP Signavio Process Management Consultant Certification.  "Managing Business Processes" is the perfect Learning Journey to dive deeper into the world of Process Management and to learn the basics about the SAP Signavio Products Process Manager, Collaboration Hub, Journey Modeler and Process Governance. Afterwards you are greatly prepared to start using the products by yourself.

Best, Cäcilia

Just joined the community after working with SAP Success Factors for a couple of years.  Going to be working with EC Payroll and Workforce Software in the coming months so a steep learning curve ahead of me.

For now though, a quick hello.

entry.title: Summary Row for a SQL HANA Query Error
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to update an SQL HANA query to include the summary row for a couple of columns.  I am using the UNION ALL method to get the summary, individually the two queries are working fine, but when executed together I get an below error.

Below query::

SELECT T2."Indicator",T0."DocNum",T0."TaxDate" "Doc Date",DAYS_BETWEEN(T0."TaxDate",CURRENT_DATE) "No. Of Days",T0."CardName", T0."NumAtCard",
cast(T3."InsTotal" as integer) AS "Total Amount",cast(T3."InsTotal"-T3."PaidToDate" as integer) AS "Open Amount", T1."SlpName" 
FROM ORDR T0 
INNER JOIN RDR6 T3 ON T0."DocEntry"=T3."DocEntry" 
INNER JOIN OSLP T1 ON T0."SlpCode" = T1."SlpCode" 
INNER JOIN OPID T2 ON T0."PIndicator" = T2."Indicator" 
WHERE DAYS_BETWEEN(T0."TaxDate",CURRENT_DATE)>365 and T0."CANCELED"='N' and T0."DocStatus"='O' 
UNION ALL
SELECT '','','','','','Total',
sum(T3."InsTotal"),sum(T3."InsTotal"-T3."PaidToDate"),''
FROM ORDR T0 
INNER JOIN RDR6 T3 ON T0."DocEntry"=T3."DocEntry" 
INNER JOIN OSLP T1 ON T0."SlpCode" = T1."SlpCode" 
INNER JOIN OPID T2 ON T0."PIndicator" = T2."Indicator" 
WHERE DAYS_BETWEEN(T0."TaxDate",CURRENT_DATE)>365 and T0."CANCELED"='N' and T0."DocStatus"='O'

on executing i am getting the below error :

SAP DBTech JDBC: [339]: invalid number: Failed to parse constant

Björn

entry.title: Questions about Individual Usage Records
entry.content:
Hi,

for a usage-based use case I have the following questions:

  • what is the optimal batch size of individual usage records that SAP Subscription Billing can accept - I know max. is 500 records, but maybe max is not the best value.
  • What is the number of parallel batches that it can handle
  • What is the processing time of each batch in SAP Subscription Billing, so I can configure reasonable response waiting timeout and to identify the processing interval, e.g. how frequently to send batches.

    Thanks

entry.title: Re: Do Virtual Tables support ST_POINT or ST_GEOMETRY
entry.content: oh, I see you are using a HANA Express (=HANA on-prem) as a remote source. Then you are right - spatial datatypes are not supported and you can workaround as you suggested via converting it to TEXT/WKT and back.

entry.title: Re: GRC Firefighter Idle Session Timeout
entry.content: Dear Ken, do you find any solution for this issue ?

entry.title: Re: Two different DM Custom View Plugins are mistakenly c...
entry.content:
Hi Kevin, thanks for your quick reply.

Of course we use your Yoeman tool which does a great job 👍
To help future beginners I would recommend documenting the attribute somewhere to clarify what it is for. In my research I have not found anything on this topic in the official documentation. In the Developer's Guide it is used a few times in code snippets but is always “false” and is never explained further. Even though the attribute name sounds descriptive, a clarification would always help me.

You have already correctly identified the problem with the second point. The problem is that the same text is displayed in the POD Designer, but these are two completely different plugins (with different i18n/builder.properties), which should have nothing to do with each other. DM is therefore incorrectly merging the Plugins here. Our test case above was that we created two plugins with the Yoeman generator. One plugin is called “peggingGraph” and one “peggingGraphZwo”. Two plugins with different names, namespaces, builder.properties, ID's etc. Nevertheless, only one text is displayed in the POD Designer (sometimes the text from the builder.properties of the first plugin is displayed, sometimes from the second). As a result, only one plugin can be added at a time in the Designer.
=> So two different plugins that are incorrectly combined

thanks, Max

entry.title: Re: Alternative to I_InventoryPriceByKeyDate
entry.content: Hi Owen, this article did not really provide an if there is an alternative to the CDS View that I need. Best, Joelle
